text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared central repository. Each time code is added, an automated build is triggered. This process compiles the new code with the existing codebase. Immediately after building, a series of tests run automatically. The main purpose of these tests is to catch issues early, often within minutes. This avoids problems that might only appear much later, when fixing them is more difficult. CI pipelines, which are the workflows defining these steps, guide the code from integration to testing. By routinely performing these checks, CI helps teams maintain a stable, healthy project while also encouraging faster development cycles.
